blocks.title: Indoor air
blocks.content:
SUBSTANCES TO INDOOR AIR,

SOIL AND WATER DURING THE

USE STAG E

The European Regulation (CE) n° 1272/2008 onclassification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures updated

by the Regulation (CE) n°790/2009 does not classify mineral wool fibres as a dangerous substance as long as they fulfil

criteria defined in its Note Q. The European Certification Board for mineral wool products, EUCEB, certify mineral wool

products made of fibres are fulfilling Note Q.www.euceb.org. The product subject of this EPD has a certificate demonstrating

compliance to Note Q. According to IARC, mineral wool insulating products are not classifiable as to carcinogenicity in

humans (group 3). The fibers are only to be expected to be released when the product is handled, during installation. Once

installed there are is no release of fibres.

ROCKWOOL insulation products for the French market have been tested and obtained a category A+ (très faibles émissions,

or very low emissions). The product subject of this EPD emits formaldehyde in concentrations < 0.0016% which is way below

the CLP threshold of 0,1%.

blocks.title: Soil and water
blocks.content:
The European Regulation (CE) n° 1272/2008 onclassification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures updated

by the Regulation (CE) n°790/2009 does not classify mineral wool fibres as a dangerous substance as long as they fulfil

criteria defined in its Note Q. The European Certification Board for mineral wool products, EUCEB, certify mineral wool

products made of fibres are fulfilling Note Q.www.euceb.org. The product subject of this EPD has a certificate demonstrating

compliance to Note Q. According to IARC, mineral wool insulating products are not classifiable as to carcinogenicity in

humans (group 3). The fibers are only to be expected to be released when the product is handled, during installation. Once

installed there are is no release of fibres.

ROCKWOOL insulation products for the French market have been tested and obtained a category A+ (très faibles émissions,

or very low emissions). The product subject of this EPD emits formaldehyde in concentrations < 0.0016% which is way below

the CLP threshold of 0,1%.

There are no emissions to soil and water during the use stage.

blocks.title: DEMONSTRATION OF VERIFICATION
blocks.content:
11 RELEASE OF DANGEROUS

SUBSTANCES TO INDOOR AIR,

SOIL AND WATER DURING THE

USE STAG E

11.1 Indoor air

The European Regulation (CE) n° 1272/2008 onclassification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures updated

by the Regulation (CE) n°790/2009 does not classify mineral wool fibres as a dangerous substance as long as they fulfil

criteria defined in its Note Q. The European Certification Board for mineral wool products, EUCEB, certify mineral wool

products made of fibres are fulfilling Note Q.www.euceb.org. The product subject of this EPD has a certificate demonstrating

compliance to Note Q. According to IARC, mineral wool insulating products are not classifiable as to carcinogenicity in

humans (group 3). The fibers are only to be expected to be released when the product is handled, during installation. Once

installed there are is no release of fibres.

ROCKWOOL insulation products for the French market have been tested and obtained a category A+ (très faibles émissions,

or very low emissions). The product subject of this EPD emits formaldehyde in concentrations < 0.0016% which is way below

the CLP threshold of 0,1%.

11.2 Soil and wate r

There are no emissions to soil and water during the use stage.

VERIFICATION

EN 15804+A2serves as the core PCR.

Independent verification of theenvironmental declaration and data according to standard EN ISO 14025:2010.
